patient_profile.name: Denise Jackson
patient_profile.lifestyle_summary: Former smoker and current heavy alcohol user with a high-salt diet. Regularly engages in moderate physical activity, and diverse social responsibilities as a midlife student.
patient_profile.past_medical_history: History of hypertension and episodic migraine headaches. No prior psychiatric conditions reported. Former years of smoking but has quit for over 10 years.
patient_profile.family_history: Mother had diabetes and difficulty sleeping in her later years. Father had hypertension. No significant psychiatric or sleep disorders noted.
patient_profile.medication_history: Currently takes lisinopril for hypertension as needed for migraines (sumatriptan). No history of using sleep aids.
patient_profile.allergy_history: No known drug or environmental allergies.
patient_profile.vaccination_history: Up to date on standard adult vaccinations including Tdap, flu, and COVID-19.

symptom_trajectory.chief_complaint: I can't seem to get a good night's sleep no matter what I do, and it's starting to affect my focus and mood.
symptom_trajectory.onset: Symptoms began gradually about four months ago with noticeable difficulty falling asleep.
symptom_trajectory.duration: Symptoms have persisted for four months.
symptom_trajectory.progression: Initially had trouble falling asleep a few nights a week but now struggles nearly every night, with additional waking up frequently in the middle of the night.

selected_symptoms.name: Long sleep-onset latency
selected_symptoms.impact: Takes over an hour to fall asleep most nights, causing frustration and anxiety.

selected_symptoms.name: Frequent nocturnal awakenings
selected_symptoms.impact: Wakes up 2-3 times a night, unable to fall asleep for 30 minutes or more each time.

selected_symptoms.name: Unrested feeling, daytime sleepiness, poor concentration, anxiety, depressed mood, or irritability
selected_symptoms.impact: Difficulty focusing on academic work and feeling irritable during social interactions.

associated_symptoms: anxiety when trying to fall asleep
negative_symptoms: severe daytime sleepiness or risk when driving
symptoms suggestive of manic or psychotic episodes
relieving_factors: sleep hygiene practices like reducing screen time before bed
engaging in relaxing activities like light stretching
symptom_trajectory.functional_impact: Unable to maintain optimal focus in studies and experiences mild strain in personal relationships due to irritability.
risk_features: Heavy alcohol use and high-salt diet may contribute to poor sleep quality, but no red-flag features like apnea or psychotic symptoms.

mental_status_exam.appearance_behavior: Neatly dressed and cooperative with normal psychomotor activity observed.
mental_status_exam.speech: Normal rate, rhythm, and tone with mildly tangential tendencies.
mental_status_exam.mood_affect: Self-reported frustration due to sleeplessness with an irritable affect becoming apparent during discussion.
mental_status_exam.thought_process: Linear and goal-directed overall, though occasionally tangent.
mental_status_exam.thought_content: No evidence of suicidal ideation, hallucination, delusions, or obsessional thinking.
mental_status_exam.perception: No abnormalities; denies hallucinations or perceptual disturbances.
mental_status_exam.cognition: Alert and oriented x4; difficulties with focus and sustained attention noted.
mental_status_exam.insight_judgment: Fair insight into sleep hygiene practices and judgment mildly impaired due to persistent alcohol use despite acknowledgment of its impact on sleep.
mental_status_exam.reliability: History appears reliable with no exaggeration or minimization of symptoms.

scale_assessments.rationale: Persisting insomnia symptoms with significant difficulties initiating and maintaining sleep, causing notable daytime impairments.
Reports of irritability, low mood, and difficulty concentrating align with moderate depressive symptoms secondary to chronic insomnia.
Consistent with pre-sleep anxiety and general restlessness exacerbated by academic stress and difficulties in sleep maintenance.

risk_assessment.rationale: Low suicide risk due to no reported suicidal ideation and functional support systems. No risk for violence or significant self-neglect beyond the impact of late-night alcohol use contributing to poor sleep patterns.

exclusionary_findings.finding: Inconsistent sleep schedule with sleep onset delay exceeding 60 minutes on most nights.
exclusionary_findings.interpretation: Supports a diagnosis of chronic insomnia related to poor sleep habits and behavioral triggers.
Likely contributing to sleep fragmentation and poor sleep quality.

exclusionary_findings.finding: No snoring, gasping, or observed apneas reported
exclusionary_findings.interpretation: Insomnia unlikely secondary to sleep apnea.

examination_results.clinical_summary: The patient, a 56-year-old African American female, presents with a 4-month history of moderate insomnia characterized by difficulty falling asleep, frequent nocturnal awakenings, and associated daytime impairments such as fatigue, poor concentration, and irritability. Key triggers include late-night alcohol use and academic stress, while sleep hygiene has shown partial efficacy in symptom management. There are no symptoms indicative of sleep apnea, manic episodes, or psychosis.
